issues.title: ToolContext is not supported by the MCP tools

comments.body:
I don't know what this mean. 

I'm already using ToolContext data in mcp tools  in my local mcp server

@Laggar, Spring AI MCP integration supports ToolContext. 

Additionally, when you add a `ToolContext` parameter to your tool method, it contains an `McpSyncServerExchange` instance under the `exchange` key. 
Access it with `McpToolUtils.getMcpExchange(toolContext)` to use logging, sampling, etc.

See this [example](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai-examples/blob/3fab8483b8deddc241b1e16b8b049616604b7767/model-context-protocol/sampling/mcp-weather-webmvc-server/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/mcp/sample/server/WeatherService.java#L59-L126) showing `exchange.loggingNotification(...)` and `exchange.createMessage(...)`.

We need to update our docs to explain these capabilities. I'll keep this issue open until then.

@tzolov I am still unable to access any data I set as context from the server. Example:

```        
val options = ToolCallingChatOptions.builder()
            .toolCallbacks(*toolCallbacks)
            .toolContext(mapOf("userId" to getUserId))
            .build()
```

And on the server, I only see the mcp client information in the exchange key. Am I missing something?

issues.title: Fix Inconsistent MCP Server Capabilities Configuration
issues.body:
Currently, the MCP Server has inconsistent handling of capabilities configuration. Capabilities are only enabled if corresponding handlers are provided during initialization, which prevents dynamically adding tools, resources, or prompts later.

### Requirements

1. Separate capability declaration from handler registration
2. Allow capabilities to be enabled independently of whether handlers are initially provided
3. Enable tools, resources, prompts, and completions to be added after server initialization
4. Add support for completions capabilities

### Expected Behavior

- Capabilities should be enabled based on configuration, not handler presence
- Server should allow registering new handlers at runtime for already enabled capabilities

issues.title: Cassandra ChatMemory Repository is unable to persist chat records
issues.body:
I am using the RC1 version. I want to persistently store my chat history using the Cassandra ChatMemory Repository that I am currently using. I have set a sufficiently long time to live. Why can't I find any records in the database after my Q&A session? Here is my code.
